diagrams.name: agent_initialization_and_startup
diagrams.title: Agent Initialization and Startup
diagrams.description: Bootstrap sequence: load tool schemas, initialise LLM session, configure system prompt, and start the agent ready to receive messages.

diagrams.name: agent_turn_processing_loop
diagrams.title: Agent Turn Processing Loop
diagrams.description: Core agentic loop: user input enters the loop, BaseHandler dispatches each turn, tool calls are resolved, and StepOutcome drives continuation or termination.

diagrams.name: llm_api_call_and_streaming_response
diagrams.title: LLM API Call and Streaming Response
diagrams.description: How a raw LLM request is built, sent to the backend (OAI-compatible relay), streamed back in chunks, and assembled into a final response with tool-use detection.
diagrams.scope: llmcore — API call and response pipeline

diagrams.name: telegram_bot_message_flow
diagrams.title: Telegram Bot Message Flow
diagrams.description: Incoming Telegram update → message handling → LLM agent call → streaming reply and abort support via tgapp handlers.

diagrams.name: feishu_lark_message_flow
diagrams.title: Feishu / Lark Bot Message Flow
diagrams.description: Feishu webhook event received → parse sender/content → download media if needed → call agent → send reply (text or card) back via Lark Open API.

diagrams.name: qq_dingtalk_wecom_bot_message_flow
diagrams.title: QQ / DingTalk / WeCom Bot Message Flow
diagrams.description: Platform-specific bot events (QQ group/c2c, DingTalk webhook, WeCom websocket) are normalised, passed to the agent, and responses dispatched back.

diagrams.name: streamlit_web_chat_flow
diagrams.title: Streamlit Web Chat Flow
diagrams.description: User submits message in the Streamlit UI (stapp / stapp2), agent is invoked, partial responses are queued and rendered progressively via session state.

diagrams.name: browser_automation_tool_execution
diagrams.title: Browser Automation Tool Execution
diagrams.description: Agent triggers a browser tool (JS execute, page scan, code_run) → TMWebDriver routes the command via WebSocket to JSExecutor in the browser → result returned to agent.

diagrams.name: memory_ocr_and_ui_detection_flow
diagrams.title: Memory, OCR, and UI Detection Flow
diagrams.description: Screen capture or ADB UI dump → OCR or UI element detection with model inference → structured results formatted for LLM context or mouse/keyboard control.

diagrams.name: autonomous_operation_and_scheduling
diagrams.title: Autonomous Operation and Scheduling
diagrams.description: Scheduler triggers a check cycle → autonomous.check decides next action → agent executes task → history helper persists operation log → session compressor archives completed sessions.
